title: Human Resources Manager
description: A Human Resources Manager in India is responsible for overseeing the recruitment, training, and development of employees, ensuring compliance with labor laws, and maintaining a positive work environment. They work closely with senior management to develop HR strategies and policies, and may handle employee grievances and conflicts. In a typical day, they may review employee performance reports, conduct interviews, and coordinate training programs.
top_private_colleges: SP Jain Institute of Management and Research
Jamnalal Bajaj Institute of Management Studies
K J Somaiya Institute of Management Studies and Research

career_progression.year_0_2: Starting as an HR Assistant, handling recruitment and employee onboarding, and assisting in policy development.
career_progression.year_3_5: Moving to an HR Coordinator role, handling employee relations, benefits, and training programs.
career_progression.year_6_10: Taking on an HR Manager position, overseeing HR functions, and developing HR strategies.
career_progression.entrepreneurship_exit: Starting an HR consulting firm or a recruitment agency.
career_progression.global_pathway: Opportunities in multinational companies, or working as an HR consultant for international clients.

india_social_context.first_year_reality: Starting salary is around ₹3-4 lakhs per annum, and working hours can be long.
